issues.title: No team directory or crew management page wired to Supabase
issues.description: The Directory page (mapped to Collaboration module) uses hardcoded mock data arrays instead of querying Supabase project_members, users, and crews tables. No real contacts, companies, or roles are displayed. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 12 requires searchable contacts grouped by company with linked RFIs, tasks, and insurance status.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Directory.tsx, in the default export component: BEFORE: The page renders a static mock array of contacts with no Supabase queries. Contact data is hardcoded inline (names like 'John Smith', 'Jane Doe' or similar placeholders). No search, no company grouping, no linked data. AFTER: Replace all mock data with a useEffect that queries supabase.from('project_members').select('id, role, user_id, users(id, first_name, last_name, email, phone, job_title, avatar_url)').eq('project_id', projectId). Add a loading skeleton (6 card placeholders with pulse animation) while data loads. Group contacts by their company/trade from the users table. Add a search input at the top that filters by name, email, phone, role, and trade (debounced 200ms). Show a contextual empty state: 'No team members yet. Invite your first team member to get started.' with an 'Invite Member' button. Each contact card should show: avatar (or initials fallback), full name, role badge with color coding per design standards (PM blue, Super amber, Sub gray), email (click to compose), phone (click to call on mobile). Ensure all data formatting uses no hyphens. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Meetings page missing agenda, action items, and attendance tracking
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Section 13 requires meeting series with agendas, collaborative minutes, action item tracking with carry-forward, and attendance management. Current page shows only a basic list with no agenda builder, no action items, and no attendance tracking. This is a core construction workflow for OAC meetings.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Meetings.tsx, in the default export component: BEFORE: The page renders a flat list of meetings with title, date, and status. No agenda section, no action items panel, no attendance roster. Clicking a meeting shows minimal detail. AFTER: Add a meeting detail panel (slides in from right, 480px width on desktop, full screen on mobile) that includes: (1) Agenda section with ordered list items, each showing title, presenter name, and allocated time. Use a simple array rendered with index numbers. (2) Action Items section showing description, assignee avatar+name, due date with color coded aging (green >4 days, amber 1 to 4 days, red overdue), and status toggle (open/completed). Query these from supabase.from('meeting_action_items') if the table exists, otherwise render from a local state array. (3) Attendance roster showing invited members from project_members with present/absent/remote status toggles (44px touch targets). (4) 'Generate Minutes' button (disabled state with tooltip 'Coming soon') placed in the panel header. Add metric cards at top of list view: Total Meetings, Upcoming This Week, Open Action Items, Overdue Action Items. Use theme.colors for all styling, no hardcoded colors. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: No AI features in collaboration module (meeting minutes, conflict detection, smart notifications)
issues.description: The collaboration module has zero AI integration. Product vision requires AI woven into every workflow. Meetings should have AI generated minutes from notes, smart action item extraction, and the AI Copilot should understand meeting context. No AI buttons or suggestions exist anywhere in this module.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/Meetings.tsx, add an AI summary feature to the meeting detail panel: BEFORE: No AI integration exists. The meeting detail panel (if it exists after C5-002 fix) has no AI buttons or suggestions. AFTER: Add an 'AI Summarize' button (sparkle icon from lucide-react, labeled 'Generate Summary') in the meeting detail panel header, next to the meeting title. When clicked, it should: (1) Set a loading state with the button showing a spinner and 'Generating...' text. (2) Call supabase.functions.invoke('ai-copilot', { body: { project_id: projectId, message: `Summarize this meeting. Title: ${meeting.title}. Date: ${meeting.meeting_date}. Attendees: ${attendeeCount}. Agenda items: ${agendaItems.map(a => a.title).join(', ')}. Notes: ${meeting.minutes || 'No notes recorded'}. Generate a concise 3 to 4 sentence summary and extract any action items.` } }). (3) On success, display the AI response in a styled card below the meeting title with a subtle purple left border (theme AI color or #8B5CF6), prefixed with a sparkle icon and 'AI Summary' label. (4) On error, show a toast notification 'Could not generate summary. Try again.' and reset the button state. (5) Add an 'AI Suggest Action Items' text button below the action items section that sends meeting notes to the copilot and displays suggested action items that the user can accept individually with a '+' button. Store accepted items in local state. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: PresenceDots and EntityPresence lack keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements
issues.description: The PresenceDots component renders avatar buttons but the overflow dropdown has no keyboard trap, no Escape to close, and no aria-expanded attribute on the trigger. The editing banner lacks role='alert' for screen reader announcement. Viewer list items inside the dropdown are div elements with no role='listitem'. WCAG 2.1 AA requires all interactive elements be keyboard accessible.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx, in the PresenceDots component and the EditingBanner component: BEFORE: The overflow button that shows '+N' viewers has no aria-expanded attribute. The dropdown panel has no role='listbox' or role='list'. Pressing Escape does not close the overflow dropdown. The EditingBanner (if present) showing 'X is editing' has no role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'. Individual viewer items in the dropdown are plain divs with no role. AFTER: (1) On the overflow toggle button, add aria-expanded={overflowOpen} and aria-haspopup='true' and aria-controls='presence-overflow-list'. (2) On the dropdown container, add id='presence-overflow-list' and role='list'. (3) On each viewer item inside the dropdown, add role='listitem'. (4) Add an onKeyDown handler to the overflow container: if Escape is pressed, close the dropdown and return focus to the overflow toggle button. (5) On the EditingBanner component (the warning that shows another user is editing), add role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' so screen readers announce it immediately when it appears. (6) Ensure all avatar buttons have aria-label formatted as '{name} is viewing this item' (not just '{name} is viewing'). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: EditConflictGuard does not surface conflict resolution UI to the user
issues.description: The useOptimisticLock hook detects conflicts and shows a warning toast, but there is no visible inline UI component that wraps form content and shows a conflict resolution banner with 'Reload latest' and 'Overwrite' options. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require edit lock conflicts show the other user's avatar with clear resolution actions, not just a transient toast.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx, after the useOptimisticLock hook definition, add or enhance an EditConflictBanner React component: BEFORE: When a conflict is detected, only a toast notification appears. There is no persistent inline banner in the form area showing who made changes and offering resolution actions. The dismissConflict function clears the state silently. AFTER: Export a new component `EditConflictBanner` that accepts props: { conflictDetected: boolean, serverUpdatedAt: string | null, onReload: () => void, onOverwrite: () => void, onDismiss: () => void }. When conflictDetected is true, render a banner with: (1) Background color using a light amber/warning tint (rgba(245, 166, 35, 0.08) with 1px solid rgba(245, 166, 35, 0.3) border). (2) AlertTriangle icon (from lucide-react, 20px, amber color #F5A623). (3) Text: 'This item was updated by another user at {formatted serverUpdatedAt time}.' (4) Two buttons: 'Reload Latest' (secondary style, calls onReload) and 'Keep My Changes' (ghost/text style, calls onOverwrite). (5) An X dismiss button in the top right corner that calls onDismiss. (6) role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' on the banner container. (7) Smooth entrance animation: opacity 0 to 1, translateY -8px to 0, 200ms ease out. (8) Banner should be 100% width, positioned at the top of the form area it wraps (not fixed/sticky to viewport). Style all elements using theme tokens from styles/theme.ts, no hardcoded colors. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

competitive_intel: Procore has a robust directory with company grouping, insurance certificate tracking, and email integration for meeting distribution. Their meetings module tracks OAC meeting series with automatic action item carry forward. Fieldwire has excellent real time presence indicators on tasks showing who is viewing. SiteSync's collaboration module is below minimum viable for a GC managing 30+ subcontractors: no real directory data, no meeting workflow depth, no AI assistance for minutes, and the edit conflict guard lacks a user facing resolution UI.
summary: The Collaboration module scores 40/100, held back primarily by empty data (directory and meetings use mock/placeholder data instead of Supabase queries), zero AI integration in any collaboration workflow, incomplete meeting management (no agenda builder, action items, or attendance), and accessibility gaps in the presence system. The edit conflict detection infrastructure exists but lacks a user facing resolution banner. The five prompts target: (1) wiring the Directory page to real Supabase data with search and company grouping, (2) building meeting detail panels with agenda, action items, and attendance, (3) adding AI summarization and action item extraction to meetings, (4) fixing keyboard navigation and ARIA attributes on presence components, and (5) creating an inline conflict resolution banner for the edit guard. These address the three lowest scoring dimensions: data richness (28), AI integration (8), and enterprise readiness (32).
